preamble: The transformation from an alveolar trill to an alveolar flap is one of the most common processes of lenition or reduction in rhotic systems, simplifying multiple rapid contacts into a single tap.

examples.note: While phonemically distinct in some positions, the trill often reduces to a tap in syllable coda or unstressed positions.
Historical development where the Latin short trill became the Spanish tap.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
